get.summary: GetCAConfig returns Central's CA certificate rendered as a Helm values YAML bundle.
get.description: This can be used to configure trust for Central when deploying secured cluster components.

get.summary: GetCRSs returns metadata for all existing Cluster Registration Secrets.

post.summary:
GenerateCRS creates a new Cluster Registration Secret with the specified name and
returns the Kubernetes Secret manifest to apply in the secured cluster.
post.description:
Returns ALREADY_EXISTS if a CRS with the given name already exists.
Returns UNIMPLEMENTED if the CRS feature is not enabled.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: CRSGenRequest is the request to generate a new Cluster Registration Secret with default settings.

post.summary:
GenerateCRSExtended creates a new Cluster Registration Secret with optional expiry and
registration limit constraints.
post.description:
Exactly one of valid_until or valid_for may be specified; providing both returns INVALID_ARGUMENT.
Returns ALREADY_EXISTS if a CRS with the given name already exists.
Returns UNIMPLEMENTED if the CRS feature is not enabled.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
CRSGenRequestExtended is the request to generate a new Cluster Registration Secret
with optional expiry and registration limits.

patch.summary: RevokeCRS revokes one or more Cluster Registration Secrets.
patch.description:
Revoked CRSs can no longer be used to register new clusters. Clusters that already completed
registration are not affected. Partial failures are reported per-ID in crs_revocation_errors.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: CRSRevokeRequest is the request to revoke one or more Cluster Registration Secrets.

get.summary:
GetInitBundles returns metadata for all existing init bundles, including which clusters
are currently authenticated using each bundle.
Deprecated: use GetCRSs instead. Init bundles are superseded by Cluster Registration Secrets.

post.summary:
GenerateInitBundle creates a new init bundle with the specified name and returns its
credentials rendered as a Helm values YAML and as Kubernetes Secret manifests.
post.description:
Returns ALREADY_EXISTS if a bundle with the given name already exists.
Deprecated: use GenerateCRS instead.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
InitBundleGenRequest is the request to generate a new init bundle.
Deprecated: use CRSGenRequest instead.

patch.summary: RevokeInitBundle revokes one or more init bundles, preventing clusters from re-authenticating using them.
patch.description:
If any of the specified bundles are currently in use by clusters, the caller must include all
affected cluster IDs in confirm_impacted_clusters_ids. The request fails if any impacted cluster
is not confirmed, returning the list of impacted clusters in the error response.

Returns ALREADY_EXISTS if the revocation conflicts, or an error if confirmation is incomplete.
Deprecated: use RevokeCRS instead.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
InitBundleRevokeRequest is the request to revoke one or more init bundles.
Deprecated: use CRSRevokeRequest instead.

CRSRevokeResponseCRSRevocationError.description: CRSRevocationError describes a revocation failure for a single CRS.

InitBundleMetaImpactedCluster.description: ImpactedCluster identifies a cluster that was registered using this init bundle.

impactedClusters.description: impacted_clusters lists the clusters still using this bundle, which must be confirmed before revoking.

InitBundleRevokeResponseInitBundleRevoca.description: InitBundleRevocationError describes a revocation failure for a single init bundle.

@type.description:
A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized
protocol buffer message. This string must contain at least
one "/" character. The last segment of the URL's path must represent
the fully qualified name of the type (as in
`path/google.protobuf.Duration`). The name should be in a canonical form
(e.g., leading "." is not accepted).

In practice, teams usually precompile into the binary all types that they
expect it to use in the context of Any. However, for URLs which use the
scheme `http`, `https`, or no scheme, one can optionally set up a type
server that maps type URLs to message definitions as follows:

* If no scheme is provided, `https` is assumed.
* An HTTP GET on the URL must yield a [google.protobuf.Type][]
  value in binary format, or produce an error.
* Applications are allowed to cache lookup results based on the
  URL, or have them precompiled into a binary to avoid any
  lookup. Therefore, binary compatibility needs to be preserved
  on changes to types. (Use versioned type names to manage
  breaking changes.)

Note: this functionality is not currently available in the official
protobuf release, and it is not used for type URLs beginning with
type.googleapis.com. As of May 2023, there are no widely used type server
implementations and no plans to implement one.

Schemes other than `http`, `https` (or the empty scheme) might be
used with implementation specific semantics.

storageUser.description: User is an object that allows us to track the roles a user is tied to, and how they logged in.

name.description: name is a human-readable label for the CRS. Must be unique across all CRSs.

v1CRSGenRequest.description: CRSGenRequest is the request to generate a new Cluster Registration Secret with default settings.

name.description: name is a human-readable label for the CRS. Must be unique across all CRSs.

validUntil.description: valid_until sets an absolute expiry time. Mutually exclusive with valid_for.

validFor.description: valid_for sets a relative validity duration from time of creation. Mutually exclusive with valid_until.

maxRegistrations.description:
4 was int32 max_registrations
max_registrations limits how many clusters may register with this CRS. Zero means unlimited.

v1CRSGenRequestExtended.description:
CRSGenRequestExtended is the request to generate a new Cluster Registration Secret
with optional expiry and registration limits.

crs.description: crs contains the Kubernetes Secret manifest (YAML) to be applied in the secured cluster.

v1CRSGenResponse.description: CRSGenResponse is returned when generating a new Cluster Registration Secret.

expiresAt.description:
expires_at is the time after which this CRS is no longer valid for new registrations.
Zero value means the CRS does not expire.

maxRegistrations.description:
max_registrations is the maximum number of clusters that may register using this CRS.
Zero value means no limit.

registrationsInitiated.description: registrations_initiated lists cluster IDs for which a registration was started but not yet completed.

registrationsCompleted.description: registrations_completed lists cluster IDs that have successfully registered using this CRS.

v1CRSMeta.description: CRSMeta holds metadata for a Cluster Registration Secret (CRS).

v1CRSMetasResponse.description: CRSMetasResponse is the response listing all Cluster Registration Secrets.

ids.description: ids lists the IDs of the CRSs to revoke.

v1CRSRevokeRequest.description: CRSRevokeRequest is the request to revoke one or more Cluster Registration Secrets.

v1CRSRevokeResponse.description: CRSRevokeResponse reports the outcome of a CRS revocation request.

helmValuesBundle.description: helm_values_bundle contains the CA certificate rendered as a Helm values YAML snippet.

v1GetCAConfigResponse.description: GetCAConfigResponse contains the Central CA configuration as a Helm values bundle.

name.description: name is a human-readable label for the init bundle. Must be unique across all init bundles.

v1InitBundleGenRequest.description:
InitBundleGenRequest is the request to generate a new init bundle.
Deprecated: use CRSGenRequest instead.

helmValuesBundle.description: helm_values_bundle contains the Helm values YAML for deploying a secured cluster with this bundle.

kubectlBundle.description: kubectl_bundle contains the Kubernetes Secret manifests for deploying a secured cluster with this bundle.

v1InitBundleGenResponse.description:
InitBundleGenResponse is the response for generating an init bundle.
Deprecated: use CRSGenResponse instead.

impactedClusters.description: impacted_clusters lists the clusters currently authenticated with this init bundle.

expiresAt.description: expires_at is the time after which this init bundle is no longer valid.

v1InitBundleMeta.description:
InitBundleMeta holds metadata for an init bundle used to authenticate a new secured cluster.
Deprecated: use CRSMeta instead. Init bundles are superseded by Cluster Registration Secrets (CRS).

v1InitBundleMetasResponse.description:
InitBundleMetasResponse is the response listing all init bundles.
Deprecated: use CRSMetasResponse instead.

ids.description: ids lists the IDs of the init bundles to revoke.

confirmImpactedClustersIds.description:
confirm_impacted_clusters_ids must contain the IDs of all clusters currently using the
bundles being revoked. The request fails if any impacted cluster ID is not confirmed.

v1InitBundleRevokeRequest.description:
InitBundleRevokeRequest is the request to revoke one or more init bundles.
Deprecated: use CRSRevokeRequest instead.

v1InitBundleRevokeResponse.description:
InitBundleRevokeResponse reports the outcome of an init bundle revocation.
Deprecated: use CRSRevokeResponse instead.
